ops.title: Yasyf rejects meta-commentary/verbose additions in docs and demands plain-language explanations instead of compressed jargon
ops.body:
During the Artifact-block planning (2026-07-12), Yasyf rejected a plan twice over prose: a SKILL.md paragraph restating what a hook already enforced ("no need for shit like this, stop adding random verbose things for no reason"), and a compressed status line ("plugin-attach + documented pack add opt-in is the new convention, with a guard test asserting no ccx pin") that he demanded be explained ("what the hell does this mean, fucking explain it, don't just say random shit").

**Why:** Meta-commentary duplicates enforcement mechanisms and rots; compressed insider shorthand forces him to reverse-engineer meaning.

**How to apply:** Before adding any doc/changelog/skill text, ask whether a mechanism already carries the message — if yes, add nothing. When reporting a repo/architecture fact, spell out the mechanism in one or two plain sentences (what changed, where, what it does) instead of naming conventions he'd have to decode. Changelog entries: one tight bullet, no disambiguating parentheticals beyond what he approved.
